  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review focus primarily on (A) scientific validity of claims vs. evidence, (B) writing quality and organization, or (C) both with emphasis on A?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Assess the internal logic of claims (e.g., interpreting larger test errors as improvements and linking gradient-norm shares to supervision dominance).",
    "R1.3": "Check whether the reported numbers and tables actually support the stated conclusions about residual weighting and generalization.",
    "R1.6": "Evaluate sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and conciseness of the section.",
    "RX.1": "Flag and contextualize uncertainty or limits in the evidence and the scope of claims.",
    "RX.2": "Guard against overgeneralizations and prescriptive recommendations that exceed what the presented results warrant.",
    "RX.3": "Keep the review within the scope of the provided section without expanding into unrelated topics or experiments.",
    "RX.4": "Consider potential downstream harm from misleading guidance to practitioners deploying PINNs.",
    "RX.5": "Maintain clear traceability by citing specific passages, figures, and numbers from the provided text when making points."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review focus primarily on (A) scientific validity of claims vs. evidence, (B) writing quality and organization, or (C) both with emphasis on A?

rationale.R1.2: Assess the internal logic of claims (e.g., interpreting larger test errors as improvements and linking gradient-norm shares to supervision dominance).
rationale.R1.3: Check whether the reported numbers and tables actually support the stated conclusions about residual weighting and generalization.
rationale.R1.6: Evaluate sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, tone, and conciseness of the section.
rationale.RX.1: Flag and contextualize uncertainty or limits in the evidence and the scope of claims.
rationale.RX.2: Guard against overgeneralizations and prescriptive recommendations that exceed what the presented results warrant.
rationale.RX.3: Keep the review within the scope of the provided section without expanding into unrelated topics or experiments.
rationale.RX.4: Consider potential downstream harm from misleading guidance to practitioners deploying PINNs.
rationale.RX.5: Maintain clear traceability by citing specific passages, figures, and numbers from the provided text when making points.
